ogTitle: Tilley Swamp vs Tolderol: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Tilley Swamp and Tolderol.
heading: Tilley Swamp vs Tolderol — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tilley Swamp, South Australia and Tolderol, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tilley Swamp has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Tolderol (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tilley Swamp or Tolderol safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tilley Swamp has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Tolderol scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tilley Swamp scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Tolderol scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tilley Swamp or Tolderol?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tilley Swamp has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
